title: 10 best herbal essential oils for hormonal imbalance
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for hormonal imbalance are natural remedies derived from plants that help to regulate and balance the body's hormonal system. They offer a holistic approach to addressing hormonal issues, such as mood swings, irregular periods, and infertility. The best herbal essential oils for hormonal balance include Vitex agnus-castus, Cimicifuga racemosa, Angelica archangelica, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Avena sativa, Lavandula angustifolia, Paeonia lactiflora, Melissa officinalis, Trifolium pratense, and Silybum marianum. If left unaddressed, hormonal imbalance can lead to anxiety, weight gain, and decreased libido, causing significant distress in daily life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it contains bioactive compounds that have a regulating effect on the endocrine system. The oil's estrogen-like properties can help to balance out the body's hormonal fluctuations, alleviating symptoms such as PMS and menopause-related hot flashes. Additionally, angelica oil has been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland, supporting the production of hormones that regulate metabolism and energy levels. By promoting hormonal equilibrium, angelica essential oils can help to reduce stress and anxiety, leading to a more balanced and healthy physiological state.
remedy_properties: Adapogenic properties: Angelica oil helps regulate hormonal balance by adapting to the body's unique needs, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to hormonal imbalances.

Estrogenic properties: Angelica oil contains estrogen-like compounds that help alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings, by supporting the body's natural estrogen production.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and related symptoms like fatigue, brain fog, and mood disturbances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to balance and regulate hormonal activity.
Rhyzomes: Contain angelic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote hormone balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica archangelica roots and leaves in early morning dew for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material in shade or air circulation for seven days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for two hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue by decanting it into a dark glass bottle.
Store the final product in a cool, dark place to preserve its bioactive compounds and aroma for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it has a profound effect on the endocrine system. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and linalool, have been shown to regulate estrogen levels and reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS and menopause. Additionally, sage oil's adaptogenic properties allow it to support the adrenal glands, promoting balance and harmony throughout the hormonal system, leading to improved overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Sage essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can disrupt hormone production and lead to imbalances, promoting a healthy hormonal environment.

Adaptogenic Properties: Sage essential oil's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress and balance cortisol levels, which is important for regulating hormones such as estrogen and progesterone.

Hormone Regulating Compounds: Sage essential oil contains compounds like thujone, camphor, and bornyl acetate that have been shown to regulate hormone production, particularly in the adrenal glands, helping to balance hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have a grounding and balancing effect on hormones, making them ideal for calming anxiety and stress associated with hormonal imbalances.
Flowers: The flowers of the sage plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier measuring 100g of plant material.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder processing 100g of dried material at once.
Set up your steam distillation apparatus with 500ml of distilled water and add the crushed salvia officinalis material to the distillation chamber processing for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillate using a paper coffee filter or glass wool and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it contains a unique compound called casticin, which has been shown to regulate hormones in the body. The oil works by stimulating the pituitary gland, which produces hormones that help balance estrogen and progesterone levels. This natural regulation can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, such as PMS, hot flashes, and mood swings. Additionally, chaste tree essential oil has been used traditionally to support women's health and fertility.
remedy_properties: Pregnenolone: This property helps regulate hormone production, particularly cortisol and estrogen levels, which can alleviate symptoms of PMS, menopause, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
Anti-inflammatory effects: By reducing inflammation in the body, chaste tree essential oil may help mitigate hormonal imbalances caused by chronic inflammation, such as endometriosis and fibroids.
Antioxidant properties: Chaste tree essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to hormone imbalance and related health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree plant are used to create essential oils that help regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly in women, by acting as a natural hormone balancer.

Flowers: Chaste tree flowers are rich in flavonoids and iridoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance, such as mood swings and hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus castus leaves and stems, ensuring proper identification and handling to avoid contamination.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation for at least seven days to reduce moisture levels.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a 10-15% ratio of plant material to distilled water for 4-5 hours to extract essential oils.
Collect and filter the essential oil using a coalescer or decanter, ensuring clear appearance and minimal particulate matter.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keeping it away from heat and light sources for optimal preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its natural ability to regulate estrogen levels, promoting a balance between progesterone and testosterone hormones. The oil contains compounds like camphor, bornyl acetate, and alpha-pinene that have been shown to reduce the production of cortisol, a hormone linked to hormonal imbalances. Additionally, sage essential oils' anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate symptoms such as PMS cramps, mood swings, and hot flashes associated with hormonal imbalance, leading to a more balanced and harmonious endocrine system.

Uterotonic: Sage essential oil has been shown to have uterotonic effects, meaning it can help regulate menstrual flow, alleviate cramps, and normalize uterine contractions, which can be beneficial for women experiencing hormonal imbalances related to menstruation or menopause.

Adaptogenic: Sage essential oil is an adaptogen, which means it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the endocrine system, including the regulation of hormones such as cortisol, estrogen, and progesterone.

Seeds: Sage seeds are a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, which help to support hormone production and balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves in morning dew for optimal potency and aroma retention.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Blend 50 grams of dried salvia sclarea leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil.
Steam distill the blended mixture in a stainless steel still for 2-3 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its calming and balancing properties, which can help regulate the body's endocrine system. The oil contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two compounds that have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, both major contributors to hormonal imbalances. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, English lavender essential oils may help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal fluctuations, such as mood swings, insomnia, and hot flashes.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating properties: English Lavender oil has been shown to regulate estrogen levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalances such as mood swings, bloating, and hot flashes.

Adrenal cortex-stimulating properties: English Lavender oil can stimulate the adrenal cortex, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate hormonal imbalances, while also supporting the body's natural production of hormones.

Antioxidant properties: English Lavender oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hormone-producing cells and disrupt hormonal balance, promoting overall endocrine health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms of PMS.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, compounds that have a calming effect on the body and can help reduce stress and anxiety related to hormonal imbalance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers and leaves from mature plants for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested botanicals using a low-temperature dehydration method within hours.
Steam distill the dried lavender material in a 12:1 ratio with distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities immediately.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black locust essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its unique composition, which includes estrogen-balancing compounds like flavonoids and saponins. These natural ingredients have been shown to gently regulate estrogen levels, reducing symptoms associated with PMS, menopause, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, black locust essential oils can also alleviate issues like mood swings, fatigue, and irregular periods. Its potent antioxidant properties further support the body's natural hormonal rhythms, leading to a more harmonious endocrine system.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic activity: The isoflavones present in black locust bark have been shown to exhibit estrogenic effects, which could potentially help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances such as hot flashes and mood swings.

Antioxidant properties: Black locust bark has antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by hormonal imbalances, promoting a healthy balance of hormone production and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The saponins present in black locust bark have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which could help alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and other discomforts associated with hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, black locust roots have been traditionally used to treat hormone-related issues such as menopausal symptoms and endometriosis.
Seeds: Black locust seeds contain a unique compound called glyasperin A, which has been shown to exhibit estrogenic properties and may help alleviate hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh robinia pseudoacacia leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried robinia pseudoacacia plant material using a still or essential oil extractor for 4-6 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil using activated charcoal and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before using it, shaking the bottle daily to facilitate blending and stabilization.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it has a direct impact on the body's endocrine system. The oil contains estrogens, which help to balance out the levels of estrogen and progesterone in the body, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and reducing symptoms of PMS such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. Additionally, fennel oil stimulates the thyroid gland, regulating metabolism and energy levels, further supporting overall hormonal balance. Antioxidant properties: Fennel essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance and related symptoms such as PMS, menopause symptoms, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).

Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a high concentration of estragole, an antioxidant that helps to normalize hormonal production and reduce symptoms of PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall, weighing about 1 pound.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator or air-drying method for at least 48 hours, maintaining temperature below 105°f.
Steam-distill the dried foeniculum vulgare material using a traditional still or a modern distillation apparatus with a steam generator, operating at 140-170°f pressure.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a glass wool pad or coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles within 24 hours of distillation, storing them in a cool, dry place.
Age the foeniculum vulgare essential oil for a minimum of 2 weeks before use, allowing its constituents to stabilize and mature for optimal therapeutic benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its ability to regulate the body's endocrine system. remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Citral's antioxidant activity helps reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hormonal imbalance by damaging cells and disrupting normal cellular function.

Anti-inflammatory: Citral's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance, such as breast tenderness and mood swings, by reducing inflammation in the body.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, bitter orange roots are used to create essential oils that help regulate hormones and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and limonoids, bitter orange seeds are prized for their ability to support hormonal balance and reduce symptoms of PMS.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried citrus x aurantium peel and organic carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil in sufficient quantities for processing.
Combine one part dried citrus aurantium peel with three parts carrier oil in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are fully covered by the oil.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to promote extraction of essential oils and resins.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible from the herbs.
Store the resulting herbal citrus x aurantium essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to six months, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its ability to regulate insulin levels and blood sugar control. The oil's active compound, cinnamaldehyde, has been shown to stimulate the production of insulin, which helps to balance hormones like cortisol, estrogen, and testosterone. Adaptogenic Properties: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's adaptogenic properties allow it to help regulate hormonal fluctuations by reducing stress and promoting a sense of balance and harmony in the body, which can be especially beneficial for women experiencing hormonal imbalances during menopause or premenstrual syndrome (PMS).

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves in the early morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with lukewarm water and mild soap to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill 100g of cleaned leaves in a 500ml distillation apparatus using 300ml of deionized water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil produced during distillation, which should yield approximately 2-3% of the original leaf material's weight.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its natural ability to regulate and soothe the endocrine system. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when they are most potent containing 15 grams of fresh leaves per 100ml.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them in filtered water for 3 minutes to remove impurities.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves using a clevenger apparatus with a temperature range of 80-90°c and a distillation time of 4 hours.
Monitor the essential oil yield and color during the distillation process ensuring a yellowish-green liquid is produced containing 15-20% essential oils.
Bottle the freshly distilled essential oil in dark glass containers and store it in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hormonal imbalance with herbal essential oils is to incorporate a blend of calming and regulating oils into your daily routine. Clary sage and bergamot promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, while lavender and ylang-ylang help to balance hormones. Use them in a diffuser or apply topically with a carrier oil to reap their benefits. Regular use can alleviate symptoms such as mood swings and hot flashes.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hormonal imbalance that are treated with herbal essential oils are stress, anxiety, and mood disorders. For example, bergamot oil is used to alleviate symptoms of depression, while lavender oil helps reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Geranium oil can help regulate menstrual cycles and ease PMS symptoms, making it a natural alternative for women's health issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it is rich in chalcones and flavonoids, which interact with the body's hormonal system to promote balance and regulation. The oils are known to stimulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, supporting the body's natural hormonal cycles. This can lead to improved menstrual regularity, reduced symptoms of PMS, and an overall decrease in hormonal stress. The oils have also been shown to have a regulating effect on the production of estrogen and progesterone, helping to alleviate symptoms of menopause and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
remedy_constituents: Agoside: Acts as a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM), which helps in regulating estrogen levels and alleviating symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance.
Vitexine: Exhibits anti-prolactin activity, thereby reducing prolactin levels in the body and helping to regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., aucubin and agnuside): Possesses anti-androgenic and anti-estrogenic properties, which help in regulating hormone levels and alleviating symptoms of hormonal imbalance, including acne, hirsutism, and menstrual irregularities.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of agnuside and ursolic acid, compounds that help regulate hormonal balance.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils due to their rich content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hormonal imbalances.

Buds: The buds are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have a regulating effect on the endocrine system and help balance hormones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitex agnus-castus flowers in the summer months when in full bloom.
Steam distill 100-200 grams of fresh or dried flowers in a 5-liter distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus by separating the oil from the water using a separator.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the final herbal vitex agnus-castus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its adaptogenic properties, which support the body's natural balance. The oil is rich in compounds like actein and cimiracemosin, which have been shown to have a positive effect on the endocrine system. By regulating hormone production and reducing stress, Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings, promoting a sense of well-being and hormonal harmony. Regular use may also support menstrual cycle balance.
remedy_constituents: Fenolics: Fenolics in Cimicifuga racemosa help stabilize hormonal balance by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in estrogen metabolism.

Triterpenoid saponins: Triterpenoid saponins in Cimicifuga racemosa help alleviate hormonal imbalances by reducing the activity of enzymes that convert androgen to estrogen, leading to a decrease in estrogen levels.

Phenolic glycosides: Phenolic glycosides in Cimicifuga racemosa help regulate the body's hormonal balance by influencing the activity of neurotransmitters and hormones, particularly those involved in the regulation of the menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cimicifuga racemosa are primarily used for their phytoestrogenic properties, which help to balance hormonal levels.
Leaves: The leaves of Cimicifuga racemosa are used for their antioxidant and estrogenic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and balance hormonal fluctuations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it contains compounds that mimic the body's natural hormones, promoting balance and regulation. remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: Helps with hormonal imbalance by acting as a neurotransmitter modulator, influencing the body's response to stress and balancing the endocrine system.
Senkyunolide A: Contributes to balancing hormonal levels by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help regulate the body's natural hormonal response.
Cariofilene: Assists in addressing hormonal imbalance by acting as a weak estrogen receptor modulator, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their hormone-regulating properties and ability to support thyroid function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 to 10 kg of fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves when they reach optimal maturity.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a distillation apparatus at 100°c to 110°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator or a centrifuge.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it has a regulating effect on the body's endocrine system. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, such as mood swings and hot flashes. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been known to help stabilize cortisol levels, which can become disrupted due to hormonal fluctuations. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce cortisol levels by inhibiting the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which in turn helps to alleviate hormonal imbalances caused by excess cortisol.

Licopyrones (isoflavonoids): These compounds have a positive effect on the hormonal balance by exhibiting estrogen-like activity, which can help to mitigate hormonal imbalances such as those experienced during menopause.

Vitexin (flavonoid): It has been shown to have a regulatory effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, thereby helping to balance hormonal levels and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help regulate hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help alleviate hormonal imbalances and promote overall hormonal balance.
Roots: They also contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, addressing hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 kg of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall after 3-4 years of cultivation.
Steam distill the dried roots in a 1:2 ratio of roots to water at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or a centrifuge within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities within 10 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

Furofuran lignans: Furofuran lignans help with hormonal imbalance by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in the metabolism of estrogen, thereby influencing estrogen levels and promoting a balance in the hormonal system.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 liter of avena sativa flowers on a sunny day in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried avena sativa flowers at a temperature of 100-150 degrees celsius for 4-6 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil in a clean container and filter it to remove any impurities.
Store the obtained avena sativa essential oil in a cool dark place in airtight glass containers for 6-12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow the body to adapt to stress and balance hormone production. The oil is rich in sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds that have a regulatory effect on the endocrine system. It is believed to influence the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, promoting a state of equilibrium between estrogen and progesterone, and other hormones, thereby supporting women's health during menstrual cycles and menopause.
remedy_constituents: Paeoniflorin: Acts as a natural estrogen receptor modulator, helping to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.
Paeonol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and balance hormonal fluctuations, particularly in the context of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
Paeonol glucoside: Shows potential in regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, contributing to a balanced hormonal environment and alleviating symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing paeonol and other compounds, the roots are used to create essential oils that help balance hormones and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.
Flowers: The flowers are a rich source of paeoniflorin, a compound believed to have a balancing effect on hormones and reduce stress and anxiety.
Buds: The buds, rich in paeonol and other bioactive compounds, are used to create essential oils that can help stabilize and balance hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the paeonia lactiflora flowers in late spring or early summer when they are fully open.
Steam distill the fresh flowers in a ratio of 1 part flowers to 4 parts water for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillate in a separate container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the herbal paeonia lactiflora essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because of its adaptogenic properties that promote emotional balance and stability. The oil's soothing and calming effects can help reduce stress, anxiety, and emotional turmoil, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances. Melissa's ability to regulate the nervous system and promote a sense of calmness can also help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS), menopause, and other hormonal-related issues, restoring balance to the body's natural rhythms. This makes it a valuable natural remedy for hormonal equilibrium.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Helps with hormonal imbalance by exhibiting anti-estrogenic properties and modulating the activity of enzymes involved in estrogen metabolism.
Luteolin: Contributes to the regulation of hormonal balance by inhibiting the production of estrogen and testosterone, and also by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in their metabolism.
Oleanolic acid: Plays a role in maintaining hormonal balance by regulating the activity of enzymes involved in the metabolism of steroid hormones, such as cortisol and aldosterone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are commonly used due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which is believed to help regulate hormonal imbalances.
Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are used for their calming and balancing effects on the body's hormonal system, particularly in women with menstrual irregularities.
Stems: Melissa officinalis stems are utilized for their high content of linalool, a compound that may help reduce anxiety and stress related to hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal oil quality.
Steam distill the melissa leaves at a temperature of 100 to 150 degrees celsius for 1 to 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity within 10 minutes.
Store the melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a dropper cap at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it is rich in isoflavones, specifically genistein and daidzein, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. These compounds can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, such as hot flashes and mood swings, by promoting balance in the body's estrogen levels. Additionally, Trifolium pratense essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and promote overall well-being, further supporting hormonal balance and reducing the symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: They help with hormonal imbalance by binding to estrogen receptors, potentially reducing menopausal symptoms and improving overall hormone balance.
Formononetin: This isoflavone is involved in the regulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which helps in maintaining a balance between various hormones in the body.

Genistein: It acts as a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM), helping to regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance, such as hot flashes and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Red Clover contain isoflavones, which help to balance estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
Leaves: The leaves of Red Clover contain phytoestrogens, which can help to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms such as hot flashes.
Roots: The roots of Red Clover contain isoflavones and other compounds that can help to regulate hormonal imbalances and improve menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of trifolium pratense flowers on a dry sunny morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 to 3 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a distillation apparatus for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils helps with hormonal imbalance because it contains compounds like silymarin, which has been shown to regulate hormonal activity in the body. It helps to balance estrogen levels, reducing the risk of conditions such as endometriosis and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Additionally, silymarin's antioxidant properties help to shield the body's hormonal system from oxidative stress, promoting a healthy balance of hormones and reducing inflammation. This helps to alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Helps with hormonal imbalance by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can protect the liver and improve its function in metabolizing hormones.
Flavonoids: Contribute to hormonal balance by exerting anti-estrogenic properties, which can help alleviate conditions like polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) and endometriosis.
Phenolic acids: Aid in hormonal balance by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hormonal imbalances in women.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Silybum marianum seeds are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high content of silymarin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Silybum marianum leaves are used to make essential oils for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help balance hormonal imbalances.
Flowers: Silybum marianum flowers are used to make essential oils for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogenic properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances in women.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh silybum marianum flowers and leaves in abundance for extraction purposes carefully.
Steam distill 50 grams of silybum marianum flowers and leaves at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water by decantation and filter through a coffee filter.
Store the extracted silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature of 15 degrees celsius.
Label the bottle with the essential oil's name and composition and store it safely away from children.
